name: Embalse de Pedrezuela – circular by Guadalix de la Sierra
name_original: Embalse de Pedrezuela – circular por Guadalix de la Sierra
region_intro: Touring cycling around San Agustín Del Guadalix offers diverse natural landscapes and terrain, located north of the Community of Madrid near the Sierra de Guadarrama. The region features the Guadalix River and its canyon, the expansive Moncalvillo meadow, and the tranquil Pedrezuela Reservoir. Over 50% of the municipal area is protected natural land, providing a varied backdrop for cycling routes that include both natural features and historical aqueducts. This area provides accessible touring cycling experiences from the capital. Best touring cycling routes around San Agustín Del Guadalix The most popular touring cycling route is Embalse de Pedrezuela – circular by Guadalix de la Sierra, a 28.8 miles (46.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 56 minutes to complete. This route offers expansive views around the Pedrezuela Reservoir. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Pedrezuela Reservoir – Hervidero Waterfall loop from San Agustín del Guadalix, a difficult 22.2 miles (35.7 km) path. This route combines reservoir views with a visit to the Hervidero Waterfall. Local touring cyclists also love the Las Cancheras Singletrack – Retuerta Aqueduct loop from San Agustín del Guadalix, a 25.2 miles (40.5 km) trail leading through varied terrain and past the impressive Acueducto de La Retuerta, often completed in about 3 hours 44 minutes. Touring cycling around San Agustín Del Guadalix is defined by the Guadalix River canyon, the Pedrezuela Reservoir, and historical aqueducts. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult routes. The routes in San Agustín Del Guadalix are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 24 reviews. More than 500 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore San Agustín Del Guadalix's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around San Agustín Del Guadalix?
A: There are over 30 touring cycling routes in San Agustín Del Guadalix, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. You'll find 5 easy routes, 13 moderate routes, and 15 difficult routes to explore.

Q: What natural landscapes can I expect to see while cycling around San Agustín Del Guadalix?
A: The region offers diverse natural landscapes, including the scenic Guadalix River canyon, the expansive Moncalvillo meadow, and the tranquil Pedrezuela Reservoir. You'll also encounter paths flanked by holm oaks, cork oaks, and Mediterranean scrub, as over 50% of the municipal area is protected natural land.

Q: Are there any waterfalls accessible by touring bike in the area?
A: Yes, the spectacular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6344971" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hervidero Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is a notable natural gem. You can visit it on routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6927843" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pedrezuela Reservoir – Hervidero Waterfall loop from San Agustín del Guadalix&lt;/a&gt;. Another waterfall, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6317937" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Coso Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, is also nearby.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or aqueducts to see along the cycling routes?
A: Yes, the area features impressive 19th-century aqueducts that are part of the Canal de Isabel II water system. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5326714" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Cancheras Singletrack – Retuerta Aqueduct loop from San Agustín del Guadalix&lt;/a&gt; will take you past the spectacular Acueducto de La Retuerta, one of the largest in the Community of Madrid. Other aqueducts like La Sima and Valcaliente also add a cultural dimension to the routes.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as loops. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e959903713/embalse-de-pedrezuela-circular-by-guadalix-de-la-sierra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Pedrezuela – circular by Guadalix de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt;, which offers expansive views around the Pedrezuela Reservoir. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10428263" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de la Constitución – Retuerta Aqueduct loop from San Agustín del Guadalix&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in San Agustín Del Guadalix?
A: San Agustín Del Guadalix is suitable for cycling throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended, while winters are mild but can be chilly.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling routes in San Agustín Del Guadalix?
A: Yes, there are easy routes suitable for families. The area around the Hervidero Waterfall, for instance, offers an easy 7 km round-trip route from San Agustín del Guadalix, ideal for family outings. The diverse terrain allows for options catering to less experienced riders.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about the routes in San Agustín Del Guadalix?
A: The komoot community rates the routes highly, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 24 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, the scenic views of the Guadalix River and Pedrezuela Reservoir, and the blend of natural beauty with historical aqueducts.

Q: Is public transport available to access the cycling routes?
A: San Agustín Del Guadalix is located north of the Community of Madrid, making it easily accessible from the capital. While specific public transport options directly to trailheads may vary, regional bus services connect the town to Madrid, from where you can start your cycling adventures.

Q: Where can I park my car when visiting the touring cycling routes?
A: Parking is generally available within San Agustín Del Guadalix itself. Many routes start from or pass through the town, offering convenient access. For specific trailheads, local parking areas are often designated, though it's always advisable to check local signage.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region offers several scenic viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/649628" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Guadalix Canyon from Matahonda Rocks&lt;/a&gt; provides stunning vistas. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3117324" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdelagua Geodetic Vertex with Views of the Sierra de Guadarrama&lt;/a&gt; offers expansive panoramic views of the surrounding mountains.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the touring cycling trails in San Agustín Del Guadalix?
A: While many natural areas in Spain are dog-friendly, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in protected natural lands or areas with wildlife. Specific regulations can vary, so check local signage or municipal guidelines for the particular route you plan to take.
tips: The Pedrezuela reservoir is a haven of peace for all those who live in the Sierra Norte de Madrid. Built by the Canal de Isabel II to take advantage of the regulated waters of the Guadalix River, it is a perfect place to stroll and unwind.

Good views of the reservoir, also known as El Vellón, with Guadalix de la Sierra in the background. The mountains that can be seen in the background are the ends of La Pedriza and the area of the Morcuera pass.

Berlanga's film "Welcome, Mister Marshal" was shot in this town in 1953. There are two churches in the film, one of which is fake, but the other is the town's own gothic church, which is well worth a visit.

A beautiful site! The small lake is perfect for disconnecting and stopping in the shade for a snack. Ideal for families.

Be careful when going down the narrow stairs that have no railings. The ascent is a bit poorly indicated, you have to look a little.

waypoints.name: Parish Church of San Juan Bautista, Guadalix de la Sierra
